title: Packages, Repositories, Dependencies, and Software Provenance · System Lens: Package Query
summary: Install or update a required tool while preserving repository trust, version evidence, rollback options, and the boundary between system and project dependencies. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux package query increment in a distribution-aware package change record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Query package origin, version, architecture, files, dependencies, reverse dependencies, and candidate changes before mutation.
Distinguish repositories, metadata, packages, dependencies, installed files, scripts, and transaction history.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Package Query
steps.instruction:
theory case — Host proposes lab-tool 2.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 2.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Package Query contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for query package origin, version, architecture, files, dependencies, reverse dependencies, and candidate changes before mutation.

steps.title: Bound Package Query
steps.instruction:
theory case — Host proposes lab-tool 3.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 3.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Package Query application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for query package origin, version, architecture, files, dependencies, reverse dependencies, and candidate changes before mutation.

content.text: Query package origin, version, architecture, files, dependencies, reverse dependencies, and candidate changes before mutation. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Host proposes lab-tool 2.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 2.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing. A defensible solution applies Package Query, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a distribution-aware package change record.

content.code:

id
stat README.md
# observation: retain exact output and exit status before changing state

steps.title: Build Package Query evidence
